title: botrychium ternatum var. pseudoternatum
intro: Botrychium ternatum var. pseudoternatum is a rare and unique type of fern, belonging to the Ophioglossaceae family. It is also known as the three-lobed grape fern, characterized by its distinctive three-lobed fronds and subterranean rhizomes. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects. The roots of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are said to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting overall well-being.
